info.name: JWT Authentication Bypass via 'alg:none'
info.description: The application accepts JSON Web Tokens signed with the 'none' algorithm. An attacker can forge a token with an arbitrary payload (e.g. an administrator identity) and strip the signature, gaining full authenticated access without credentials.

info.name: Arbitrary File Read via Path Traversal (/ftp)
info.description: The /ftp endpoint serves files and is vulnerable to directory traversal using an encoded poison null byte (e.g. ../ sequences with %2500). An unauthenticated attacker can read files outside the intended directory, including application source and configuration with embedded secrets.

info.name: SQL Injection Authentication Bypass in Login
info.description: The login form concatenates the email parameter directly into a SQL query. Submitting a payload such as ' OR 1=1-- returns the first user record and authenticates the attacker as that account (typically the administrator).

info.name: lodash <4.17.12 Prototype Pollution (CVE-2019-10744)
info.description: The bundled lodash library is older than 4.17.12 and is vulnerable to prototype pollution via defaultsDeep. An attacker can inject properties onto Object.prototype, leading to denial of service or, in some flows, property-injection that escalates to remote code execution.

info.name: Reflected Cross-Site Scripting in Product Search
info.description: The product search endpoint reflects the q parameter into the DOM without output encoding, allowing arbitrary JavaScript execution in the victim's browser (e.g. <iframe src="javascript:alert(`xss`)">).

info.name: CORS Misconfiguration — Reflected Origin with Credentials
info.description: The application reflects the request Origin header into Access-Control-Allow-Origin while also setting Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true. Any malicious site can issue authenticated cross-origin requests and read the responses on behalf of a logged-in victim.

info.name: Exposed Prometheus Metrics Endpoint
info.description: The /metrics endpoint is publicly accessible and exposes internal application and runtime telemetry (route names, process memory, event-loop lag, library versions). This leaks implementation detail useful for targeting further attacks.

info.name: Outdated JavaScript Library — jQuery 2.2.4
info.description: The front end loads jQuery 2.2.4, which has multiple known cross-site scripting vulnerabilities in $.html() and cross-domain ajax handling. Upgrade to a current 3.x release.

info.name: Application Configuration Disclosed
info.description: The /rest/admin/application-configuration endpoint returns the full application configuration object without authentication, revealing feature flags, challenge settings, and internal application behavior.

info.name: Missing Security Headers
info.description: Responses are served without a Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, or Strict-Transport-Security header, reducing defence-in-depth against clickjacking, content injection, and protocol downgrade.

info.name: Server Technology Disclosure (X-Powered-By: Express)
info.description: The X-Powered-By response header reveals the Express framework, helping an attacker fingerprint the stack and narrow exploit selection. Disable the header (app.disable('x-powered-by')).

info.name: robots.txt Disclosed
info.description: A robots.txt file was located. It references a /ftp path that hints at a publicly reachable file directory.

info.name: Email Addresses Disclosed
info.description: Product reviews and the customer feedback API return registered user email addresses, exposing personal data that can be used for phishing or credential-stuffing.
